This kernel appears to be a synthetic/contrived microbenchmark rather than something from a known codebase or tutorial. The specific characteristics — arbitrary prime-number strides (17, 257), bitwise AND masking with 4095 (wrapping to a 4096-element buffer), an unrolled loop with a multiply-add accumulation using the constant 1.1f — look like they were hand-crafted to test specific memory access patterns or cache behavior. It doesn't match any well-known benchmark suite (e.g., Rodinia, Parboil, SHOC) or standard CUDA tutorial code that I'm aware of.
